description:
Turn the Terms refund scenarios into an explicit Stripe action matrix and local evidence contract before implementing broad refund handling.

**Scope**
- Map launch refund scenarios from the Terms to concrete outcomes: before clinician review, clinical denial, after visit but before pharmacy shipment, after shipment, damaged/lost shipment, and clinical discontinuation.
- Decide which scenarios are automated, which require support/ops approval, and which require MDI/pharmacy status confirmation.
- Define allowed Stripe actions for each scenario: no-op, full refund, partial refund, credit, cancel-only, or manual review.
- Specify the local evidence records and dashboard/support messaging for refund pending, approved, denied, completed, or failed states.
- Keep Stripe metadata, evidence metadata, logs, and support-facing records free of PHI, clinical reasons, medication names, questionnaire answers, clinician notes, and free-text health context.

**Acceptance**
- Refund matrix tests map each Terms scenario to an allowed Stripe action and patient-safe status.
- Scenarios that depend on pharmacy shipment or clinical judgment fail closed to manual review until authoritative MDI/pharmacy state is available.
- Stripe refund/dispute webhooks update only bounded local mirror/evidence state and do not overwrite billing linkage unexpectedly.
- Dashboard/support copy communicates refund state without exposing clinical detail.
- The matrix is reviewed against T-077 legal copy before launch automation is enabled.
title: Refund matrix and Stripe action contract
